_comment: Issue #1991 agentic blocked-prompt resolver — CLOSED, default-deny action policy.
This is the SHIPPED, source-controlled allow-list the v0.16.16 canary resolver
- Default-deny: a prompt_kind NOT listed here, or listed with enabled=false, is
NEVER auto-actioned. The resolver denies and lets the #1992 safety floor escalate.
- An install-local override file may only DEMOTE a row to deny/report-only. It may
NEVER promote a new auto-action or enable a denied kind (the resolver merges with
- 'keys' are SEMANTIC tokens only, drawn from the closed vocabulary that
refuses any token outside that set. Pane text NEVER supplies a key token.
- required_confidence gates which detector confidence is allowed to auto-action.
'low' / 'unknown' confidence never matches a high-required row.
billing / usage / plan-upgrade / permission / overwrite / feedback / context_pressure /
unknown_interactive are explicitly DENY for v0.16.16 — they are never auto-actioned.

required_fragments: I am using this for local development
actions.note: Select option 1 'I am using this for local development'. Replaces the controller/agent dev-channels auto-accept under the single owner.
Allowed ONLY when the target is a bridge-registered agent session AND the live session workdir equals the registered agent workdir. Otherwise deny.
Safe resume path only ('Resume from summary (recommended)', option 1). Never choose 'full session as-is' by text inference.
DENY in the initial canary. No free-form digit passthrough; escalate.
DENY. Ambiguous context-pressure modal — never blindly press Enter; escalate.
DENY. No auto-yes / blanket approval / session permission grant. Escalate.
DENY, even 'Stop and wait for limit to reset'. Usage/billing surfaces are never auto-actioned by the resolver. Escalate.
DENY. Unknown structured picker — escalate (the floor's longer unknown deadline applies).
